Title: TRANSPARENT TESTA 16 collaborates with the MYB-bHLH-WD40 transcriptional complex to produce brown fiber cotton

Common_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: Ys / Handan208 allelic context not specified
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Developing fiber (5–15 DPA); also assayed in leaves by VIGS
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pigment deposition causing brown fiber; antioxidant and protective flavonoid functions discussed
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively associated with proanthocyanidin accumulation and brown fiber pigmentation as a component of the MBW complex; silencing reduces PA content
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Constitutively expressed in roots, stems, leaves, petals, anthers, ovules, and fibers; higher in 5–10 DPA brown fiber than white fiber
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Constitutive across tissues; elevated in early developing brown fiber
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; LCI; BiFC; GST pull-down with Gh_TT2; VIGS; RT-qPCR; PA quantification
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Gh_WD physically interacts with Gh_TT2 and participates in the cotton MBW complex. 2. Gh_WD expression is elevated in early brown fiber development. 3. VIGS silencing of Gh_WD lowers PA content, supporting a positive role in PA biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: TRV-based VIGS in cotton; no stable promoter-specific construct as focal evidence
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential co-engineering partner with Gh_TT2/Gh_TT8 to enhance brown fiber pigmentation, but likely requires coordinated expression rather than single-gene deployment
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Classified here as Common_Gene because it is a WD40 scaffold/cofactor rather than catalytic enzyme or primary upstream TF; it promotes Gh_TT2–Gh_WD interaction with Gh_TT8 in Y3H context.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Ys; XC1; cotton callus protoplast system
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Brown fiber pigmentation and protective flavonoid function
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses p-coumaroyl-CoA with malonyl-CoA units to form naringenin chalcone
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key early structural gene, but not established as rate-limiting in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promoter is activated by Gh_TT2 and more strongly by Gh_TT2 + Gh_TT8, supporting increased PA biosynthetic flux
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Included among key enzyme genes with higher expression at 5–10 DPA in dark brown fiber
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Dual-LUC promoter activation assays; transcriptome/WGCNA correlation
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Gh_TT2; Gh_TT8; Gh_TT16 (indirect negative effect via promoter activity context)
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CHS promoter is positively regulated by Gh_TT2. 2. Gh_TT8 enhances Gh_TT2-mediated CHS activation. 3. GhTT16 weakens Gh_TT2 activation of CHS, linking CHS transcription to the upstream regulatory network.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Treated as core pathway gene because its promoter was directly assayed as a Gh_TT2/Gh_TT8/GhTT16 target in Results.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Ys; XC1; cotton callus protoplast system
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Brown fiber pigmentation and flavonoid defense functions
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces leucoanthocyanidins to catechin-type flavan-3-ols feeding PA formation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key branch enzyme; not explicitly rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promoter is activated by Gh_TT2 and further promoted with Gh_TT8; higher expression is associated with stronger brown fiber pigmentation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Marked differential expression between Ys and XC1; relatively high in EM compared with anthocyanin-biased lines
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Dual-LUC promoter assays; RNA-seq heatmap comparison
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LAR is a major downstream target of Gh_TT2 in the PA pathway. 2. Gh_TT8 enhances the positive effect of Gh_TT2 on LAR promoter activity. 3. Higher LAR expression correlates with PA-biased brown fiber coloration.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper highlights LAR as one of the most divergent structural genes between dark and light brown cotton and between Gh_TT2- versus Re-driven color states.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Ys; XC1; cotton callus protoplast system
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Brown fiber pigmentation and flavonoid defense functions
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces anthocyanidins to epicatechin-type flavan-3-ols that polymerize into PAs
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key branch enzyme; not explicitly rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Gh_TT2 activates the ANR promoter, supporting PA accumulation; GhTT16 suppresses this activation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Differentially expressed across materials; relatively elevated in PA-biased lines
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ANR is directly promoted by Gh_TT2 in transactivation assays. 2. GhTT16 weakens Gh_TT2-mediated ANR activation. 3. ANR expression pattern supports a shift toward PA synthesis in brown fiber.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: ANR and LAR together are emphasized as determinants of PA/anthocyanidin balance in the model.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Ys; XC1; cotton callus protoplast system
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Controls branch competition between anthocyanidin and PA flux, affecting fiber color
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Anthocyanidin branch point connected to PA precursor pool
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Competes with target-product branch / supplies precursor to ANR
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Branch-switch enzyme context; not directly called rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Gh_TT2 alone attenuates ANS promoter activity, while Gh_TT8 mitigates this repression; GhTT16 counteracts Gh_TT2 effects, influencing pigment ratio
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Relatively lower in Gh_TT2-overexpressing PA-biased material than in Re-related lines
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Gh_TT2; Gh_TT8; GhTT16; Re (model-level competing regulator)
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ANS is differentially regulated from other tested pathway genes. 2. Gh_TT2 represses or attenuates ANS promoter activity, unlike its activation of CHS/LAR/ANR. 3. Gh_TT8 alleviates Gh_TT2-mediated ANS inhibition, supporting branch-balance control between anthocyanidin and PA synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Central to the proposed competition between Gh_TT2-centered PA regulation and Re-mediated anthocyanin regulation.

Regulation_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: Ys allele derived from interspecific hybridization involving Gossypium barbadense acc. Pima 90-53 and Gossypium hirsutum acc. Handan208; comparator allele from Handan208
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Brown fiber pigmentation; protective antioxidant/anti-UV/anti-pest functions of PAs discussed
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation of PA pathway genes; repression/attenuation of ANS; MBW complex-dependent regulation
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Proanthocyanidin biosynthesis and pigment deposition in cotton fiber
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates CHS, LAR, and ANR promoters; attenuates ANS promoter activity
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Causal gene for brown fiber cotton and key MYB determinant of PA deposition
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: High Gh_TT2 expression drives PA accumulation and produces brown fiber; Ys allele has stronger effect than Handan208 allele
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Specifically high in 5–15 DPA fiber; much higher in Ys than in Handan208
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Fiber-specific early/mid development expression; promoter GUS signal in 0 DPA ovule, 3 DPA ovule, and 5–15 DPA fibers
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Promoter sequence variation including TATA-box indel; repression by GhTT16
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms MYB-bHLH-WD40 complex with Gh_TT8 and Gh_WD
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Gh_TT8; Gh_bHLH48; Gh_WD; GhTT16 (upstream promoter-binding regulator)
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression analysis; transgenic overexpression under PGbEXPA2 in cotton; PA quantification; promoter dissection/LUC; subcellular localization; Y2H; LCI; BiFC; GST pull-down; Y3H; dual-LUC target promoter assays; transcriptomics/WGCNA
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Gh_TT2 is the causal gene for brown fiber cotton and promotes PA accumulation in developing fiber. 2. Both Ys and Handan208 cDNAs can induce light brown fiber when overexpressed, but the Ys allele has stronger downstream activation. 3. Promoter variation, especially a TATA-box indel, contributes to gain-of-function expression differences. 4. Gh_TT2 acts in an MBW complex with Gh_TT8 and Gh_WD to regulate PA structural genes.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: PGbEXPA2 fiber elongation-specific promoter for overexpression; native Gh_TT2 promoters ProYs and ProHD208 in reporter assays
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression generated light brown rather than dark brown fiber, indicating single-gene activation may be insufficient for maximal pigmentation or may alter pigment balance
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for breeding and fiber color engineering; promoter optimization or activation, combined with homologous PABG activation and MBW partners, may enhance brown color while balancing anthocyanin/PA ratios
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Coding SNP changes Pro→Thr and strengthens regulatory output, but promoter variation appears central to elevated expression; expression is not altered in nuclear localization by coding SNP.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Ys; Handan208; cotton callus protoplast system
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Brown fiber pigmentation and flavonoid protection
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: MBW complex co-activation; promotes interaction between Gh_TT2 and Gh_WD; modulates promoter-specific effects
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Proanthocyanidin biosynthesis and branch balance with anthocyanidin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: CHS; LAR; ANS; likely ANR in MBW context
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Enhances Gh_TT2 activation of CHS and LAR; attenuates Gh_TT2 inhibition on ANS; promotes MBW complex formation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential MBW partner that strengthens Gh_TT2 regulatory output on PA biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports PA pathway activation and influences pigment flux partitioning in brown fiber
Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus (in interaction assays, inferred as TF; direct localization not explicitly stated)
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Significantly higher in brown fiber cotton than white fiber cotton across various fiber developmental stages except 25 DPA; trend similar to Gh_TT2
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Elevated during fiber development in BFC
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: MYB-bHLH-WD40 complex with Gh_TT2 and Gh_WD
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; LCI; BiFC; Y3H; expression profiling; dual-LUC promoter assays; transcriptome/WGCNA
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Gh_TT8 physically interacts with Gh_TT2. 2. Gh_TT8 promotes the Gh_TT2–Gh_WD interaction, supporting MBW complex assembly. 3. Gh_TT8 enhances Gh_TT2-mediated activation of CHS and LAR and alleviates Gh_TT2 repression of ANS.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Because Gh_TT8 can promote ANS expression, its effect may also alter anthocyanidin/PA balance depending on partner proteins such as Re or Gh_TT2
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful co-factor for stacking with Gh_TT2 to strengthen brown fiber pigmentation, but branch-balance effects on ANS should be monitored
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Chosen over Gh_bHLH48 for follow-up due to expression pattern more consistent with Gh_TT2.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Ys; Handan208; cotton protoplasts; N. benthamiana reporter system
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Modulates brown fiber pigmentation through PA pathway repression
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional repression / negative modulation of Gh_TT2 and PA pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Upstream control of MBW complex activity and PA biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Gh_TT2 promoter; CHS; DFR; LAR; ANR; ANS (directly or via weakening Gh_TT2 effects)
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Binds the Gh_TT2 promoter and suppresses its expression; weakens Gh_TT2-mediated activation of CHS, DFR, LAR, and ANR; counteracts Gh_TT2 effect on ANS
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Negative upstream regulator that restrains PA accumulation by limiting Gh_TT2/MBW output
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Inhibits Gh_TT2 and negatively modulates PA pathway activity, thereby influencing brown fiber pigmentation
Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus (inferred for MADS-box TF; not directly reported)
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Shows opposite expression trend to Gh_TT2 in transcriptome data
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Contrasting trend versus Gh_TT2 across 5–15 DPA fibers
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Not shown as MBW component; acts upstream and independently on pathway gene promoters
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y1H; LUC reporter assays in protoplasts and N. benthamiana; EMSA; transcriptome expression comparison
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhTT16 binds the Gh_TT2 promoter via CArG-box-containing regions. 2. GhTT16 represses Gh_TT2 promoter activity. 3. GhTT16 weakens Gh_TT2 regulation of multiple PA pathway genes and can also directly regulate PABGs. 4. GhTT16 expression is opposite to Gh_TT2, supporting an inhibitory role in brown fiber pigmentation.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Gh_TT2 promoter fragments in LUC assays; 35S-driven GhTT16 effector
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Downregulating GhTT16 may increase pigmentation but could perturb broader developmental functions associated with BSISTER/MADS factors; not tested here
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A promising repression target for engineering stronger brown fiber via release of Gh_TT2 and PA pathway activity
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper title emphasizes its collaboration with MBW complex, but mechanistically the Results support mainly negative modulation of Gh_TT2/MBW activity rather than membership in the complex.
